milestones.title: Planning and contract freeze
milestones.summary: The planning and contract-freeze set is now the governed source of truth for browser and Track B promotion work, with milestone state tracked centrally in this manifest.

evidence.description: Primary milestone and acceptance plan.
Track A selector, kill switch, denylist, and fallback contract.
Shared module matrix for Track A and Track B.

exitCriteria: Runtime seam contract is frozen enough for implementation slices.
Gate mapping and rollback policy are documented.
Track B module boundaries are drafted before code promotion.
nextActions: Keep the contract set synchronized with future browser and Track B governance changes.
Advance this manifest whenever any milestone evidence or ownership contract changes.

milestones.title: Track A prototype (flagged, fallback first)
milestones.summary: Track A runtime seam bring-up is now governed by a core browser gate with explicit ownership, promotion approvals, and fresh macOS smoke evidence for both Dawn and Doe.

evidence.description: Bring-up flow and lane wrapper usage.
Track A selector and decoder seam work log.
Local Chromium lane bring-up and selector status snapshot.
Lane dependency and resolved-path preflight.
Linux bring-up wrapper for Chromium lane setup/build.
Latest local browser-layered summary artifact showing Doe vs Dawn execution in the lane.
Fresh promoted-browser-gate smoke artifact showing Dawn and Doe both pass browser WebGPU smoke.
Promoted ownership assignment for Track A (browser) runtime/quality/methodology scope.
Core Track A (browser) gate using the lane wrappers for preflight and Playwright evidence.
Fresh Track A browser gate report proving governed preflight and smoke execution on this host.

exitCriteria: Flagged Doe selection launches Chromium workloads without changing Dawn-default behavior.
Fallback reasons are observable and deterministic.
Unsupported or denylisted configurations fail closed to Dawn unless Doe is explicitly forced.
nextActions: Keep the promoted browser gate green on macOS host refreshes.
Promote any future selector/fallback contract changes through the ownership and approval path before advancing artifact hashes.

milestones.title: Track A compatibility hardening
milestones.summary: Track A compatibility hardening is now governed by the promoted browser gate: the strict 80-row layered browser run passes with zero required failures on both Dawn and Doe under required promotion approvals.

evidence.description: L0/L1/L2 browser projection and promotion contract.
Generated browser projection manifest from core workloads.
L2 browser workflow definitions.
Projection and coverage checker for browser-layered runs.
Latest strict promoted-browser-gate checker artifact for the current 80-row projection.
Fresh March 9 browser-gate status note with promoted strict-run outcomes.
Promoted Track A browser approval manifest with runtime, quality, methodology, and coordinator signoff.
Core Track A browser gate report covering fresh strict layered validation.

exitCriteria: Required L1 and L2 browser rows report explicit typed status and statusCode.
Projection and workflow hashes stay synchronized with core workloads.
Trace and compatibility evidence can be refreshed by script rather than manual curation.
nextActions: Add CTS/browser-suite artifact paths when those runs exist so the governed gate can expand beyond the current smoke plus layered superset corpus.
Keep workflow-manifest and approval contracts in sync with the promoted browser gate.

milestones.title: Track A performance qualification
milestones.summary: Track A browser performance qualification is now governed: fresh smoke and layered artifacts are produced by the promoted browser gate and still classify browser performance as diagnostic rather than claimable.

evidence.description: One-command browser-layered benchmark wrapper.
Generate-run-check-summary orchestration for the browser lane.
Latest promoted-browser-gate layered performance summary; still diagnostic by contract.
Fresh promoted-browser-gate smoke performance proxy artifact; still diagnostic by contract.
Core Track A browser gate report proving fresh smoke and layered performance artifacts were generated under governance.

exitCriteria: Browser reports state diagnostic vs claimable status explicitly.
No browser-layered artifact is misrepresented as an L0 release claim.
Timing source and comparability scope stay explicit in generated summaries.
nextActions: Keep browser artifacts out of canonical claim paths unless a formal browser claim lane is added.
Treat the promoted browser gate as governance/correctness evidence only until browser claim methodology is explicitly approved.

milestones.title: Track B module 1 (fawn_2d_sdf_renderer) incubation
milestones.summary: The first Track B module now has both the nursery prototype lane and a promoted core Zig implementation with canonical config, ownership, and blocking gate coverage.

evidence.description: Candidate input/output/fallback contract for the first Track B module.
Config-driven thresholds and allowed operation policy for Track B (modules) prototypes.
Schema-backed request/result contract for the SDF renderer prototype.
Deterministic SDF renderer prototype request fixture.
Prototype gate for all Track B (modules) fixtures.
Passing Track B prototype gate report with deterministic fixture results.
Canonical promoted schema for the SDF renderer.
Canonical promoted policy for the SDF renderer.
Blocking core Track B (modules) gate for promoted modules.

exitCriteria: A deterministic fixture lane exists for the SDF renderer.
Fallback reasons are typed and traceable.
CPU-owned semantics remain outside the module boundary.
nextActions: Keep expanding the fixture corpus beyond the single deterministic request.
Wire the promoted SDF module into browser-owned call sites only after Track A seam governance is ready.

milestones.title: Track B additional modules (one-by-one)
milestones.summary: All additional Track B modules now have schema-backed deterministic prototype fixtures plus promoted core schemas, policies, ownership, and blocking gate coverage.

evidence.description: Path engine contract draft.
Effects pipeline contract draft.
Compute services contract draft.
Resource scheduler contract draft.
Schema-backed path engine request/result contract.
Schema-backed effects pipeline request/result contract.
Schema-backed compute services request/result contract.
Schema-backed resource scheduler request/result contract.
Deterministic path engine prototype request fixture.
Deterministic effects pipeline prototype request fixture.
Deterministic compute services prototype request fixture.
Deterministic resource scheduler prototype request fixture.

Passing Track B prototype gate report covering all additional modules.

Canonical promoted effects pipeline policy.

Canonical promoted path engine policy.

Canonical promoted resource scheduler policy.

exitCriteria: Each module has an independent artifact lane and kill switch.
No module skips schema/correctness/trace gates.
Each module reports claimable vs diagnostic status explicitly.
nextActions: Add richer per-module fixture corpora and explicit fallback fixtures.
Delay browser-owned call-site rollout until browser seam governance is ready.

milestones.title: Promotion and maintenance
milestones.summary: All five Track B modules now have promoted core schemas and policies, explicit ownership, deterministic tests, blocking gate coverage, and explicit promotion approvals.

evidence.description: Promotion approval artifact schema.
Explicit promotion approvals state.
Ownership schema for promoted Track B (modules).
Owners, promotion dates, and nursery-exit approvals for promoted Track B (modules).
Blocking schema/correctness/trace gate for all promoted Track B (modules).
Migration notes for promoted Track B (modules).
Current Fawn status doc with promoted Track B (modules) ownership and gate state.

exitCriteria: Promoted behavior is no longer dependent on nursery-only assumptions.
Required gate wiring is defined in core directories.
Rollback and ownership are explicit before promotion.
nextActions: Keep browser-facing rollout separate from promoted core ownership and gate coverage.
Add advisory Track B performance coverage before making any claimability statements.
